Title: Expansion of the Catalytic Repertoire of Alcohol Dehydrogenases in Plant Metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Medium-chain alcohol dehydrogenase (ADH), NAD(P)H-dependent oxidoreductase, Rossmann-fold ADH
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis; vinblastine biosynthesis; ibogaine-related alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream branch-point and downstream reductive tailoring step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate (proposed unstable intermediate) leading to dehydrosecodine; secodine leading to vincadifformine; 19,20-dihydrovallesiachotamine from strictosidine aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes non-canonical 1,4-reduction of an α,β-unsaturated iminium moiety in precondylocarpine acetate at C19; can catalyze a second non-stereoselective 1,4-reduction of dehydrosecodine at C15; also catalyzes 1,4-reduction of the α,β-unsaturated carbonyl in strictosidine aglycone at C19
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate unstable intermediate and downstream branch products leading to alkaloid scaffolds; directly promotes formation of vincadifformine-related products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch/non-canonical reductive step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of dehydrosecodine-derived monoterpene indole alkaloid scaffolds and can generate vincadifformine via double 1,4-reduction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized plant natural products; vinblastine/iboga alkaloid biosynthetic intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays with NADPH/NADPD; acidic conditions trap angryline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Deuterium-labeling with 4-pro-R-NADPD and NMR; in vitro enzyme assays; X-ray crystallography; site-directed mutagenesis; product characterization by NMR/CD
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate/product instability limited steady-state kinetic quantification; active-site mutations alter product ratio and promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 1. CrDPAS and TiDPAS2 structures lacked catalytic zinc but retained structural zinc. 2. 1,4-reduction does not require canonical catalytic zinc or proton relay. 3. Restoring zinc-coordinating residues altered product distribution but did not abolish activity. 4. DPAS also turns over strictosidine aglycone, showing catalytic plasticity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DPAS is the first reported ADH shown to catalyze 1,4-reduction of an α,β-unsaturated iminium. 2. Isotopic labeling established hydride incorporation at C19 of precondylocarpine acetate-derived product and at C15 in the second reduction leading to vincadifformine. 3. Structural analysis showed loss of catalytic zinc and dispensability of the canonical proton relay for this reaction. 4. DPAS displays substrate promiscuity and active-site plasticity toward other MIA intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway engineering and biotechnological diversification of monoterpene indole alkaloids by installing non-canonical reductive steps; active-site motifs may guide enzyme discovery/engineering

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis; vinblastine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Strictosidine aglycone reduction branch toward geissoschizine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes irreversible 1,2-iminium reduction of strictosidine aglycone to geissoschizine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate leading toward vinblastine-related alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-defining reductive step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes geissoschizine formation from strictosidine aglycone; can also reduce precondylocarpine acetate with much lower efficiency than DPAS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized plant natural products; intermediate in vinblastine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays with NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystallography; site-directed mutagenesis of catalytic zinc and proton relay residues; enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytic zinc mutations abolish activity; lower efficiency on non-native substrate precondylocarpine acetate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GS retains catalytic and structural zinc but lacks the canonical proton relay; data support catalytic zinc requirement for 1,2-iminium reduction but proton relay dispensability.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GS catalyzes non-canonical 1,2-iminium reduction of strictosidine aglycone to geissoschizine. 2. Crystal structure and mutagenesis showed catalytic zinc is essential. 3. Canonical proton relay residues are altered and not required for catalysis. 4. GS has limited promiscuous activity toward precondylocarpine acetate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be exploited for engineering MIA branch chemistry and for motif-based discovery of iminium-reducing ADHs

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternate strictosidine aglycone isomer reduction branch toward tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 1,2-iminium reduction of an alternate strictosidine aglycone isomer to form tetrahydroalstonine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms branch product/intermediate in alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-defining reductive step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays referenced from prior work
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Structural comparison with previously solved apo/holo structures; mutational comparison referenced in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a comparative, experimentally characterized atypical ADH. Retains catalytic zinc but lacks canonical proton relay; Tyr56 hydroxyl not required for catalysis according to comparative mutagenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAS is another plant ADH catalyzing non-canonical 1,2-iminium reduction. 2. Structural comparison indicates catalytic zinc is retained, whereas canonical proton relay is altered. 3. Comparative mutagenesis suggests the hydroxyl-bearing residue in the canonical relay is not required for catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparator and candidate for engineering alternative MIA branch products

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Canonical medium-chain alcohol dehydrogenase (ADH), NAD(P)H-dependent oxidoreductase
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Corresponding aldehyde/alcohol partner in reversible redox reaction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible oxidation/reduction of 8-hydroxygeraniol and corresponding aldehyde substrate as a canonical 1,2-aldehyde/alcohol ADH reaction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies upstream precursor pool for MIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as canonical ADH comparator illustrating standard catalytic zinc- and proton relay-dependent aldehyde reduction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor formation for specialized alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Previously reported crystal structure used for structural comparison
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a canonical reference rather than primary focus of experimentation in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 8HGO represents the canonical ADH mechanism with catalytic zinc and proton relay. 2. Structural comparison with DPAS and GS highlights how active-site changes enable atypical reductions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reference scaffold for engineering canonical versus non-canonical ADH activities
